description:
Goal: keep compiler2's downstream function consumers alive after the source-authority swap by deriving an honest `legacy_fn_def`-style artifact from grouped quoted function roots, not from the old parser or `Rc<Item>` / parser-built `FnDef` authority.

Grounding:
- `src/compiler2/jobs/contract.rs`, `src/compiler2/jobs/body.rs`, `src/compiler2/jobs/dispatch.rs`, and `src/compiler2/jobs/keying.rs` still consume the old `FnDef`/`Expr`/`Pattern` shape today.
- fz-rh2.11.7.13 fixes the original sin upstream by making grouped quoted function source first-class and stable; once that lands, compiler2 can derive the old Rust-shaped `FnDef` from the grouped quoted root without reparsing or borrowing parser authority.
- Porting every downstream consumer directly to quoted readers is a larger follow-on. Until then, a single compiler2-owned derivation seam is the honest transitional shape.

Signal:
- compiler2 never reparses source text through the old frontend in order to feed contract/body/dispatch/keying
- downstream jobs obtain an explicitly named `legacy_fn_def` artifact (or equivalent honest carrier) derived from the grouped quoted function root
- function/body/dispatch semantic facts remain stable when the derived downstream artifact is unchanged, with source authority still rooted in `{heap, root}`
- naming/docs make it obvious this is transitional legacy surface, not canonical source authority

Change:
- add a one-way compiler2-owned grouped-quoted -> legacy function artifact derivation path
- cache or fact it at the function tier so downstream jobs share one derived artifact instead of each growing their own reader/converter
- route contract/body/dispatch/keying through that artifact while keeping quoted source as the only authoritative function source record
- add focused tests around revision behavior and no-old-parser usage

Why:
- this preserves one source authority while buying a clean migration seam for the deepest remaining old-AST consumers
- the stinky name is intentional: we should be able to grep for the debt later and rip it out deliberately

comments.text: Follow-on clarification after the Elixir review: this ticket should consume the compiler-owned grouped quoted roots produced by fz-rh2.11.7.13. It should not regrow grouping logic, and it should not ask the parser/frontdoor for a grouped function artifact. The only job here is one-way derivation of the transitional  artifact from those already-grouped quoted roots.

Follow-on clarification after the Elixir review, replacing the shell-mangled note above:

This ticket should consume the compiler-owned grouped quoted roots produced by `fz-rh2.11.7.13`. It should not regrow grouping logic, and it should not ask the parser/frontdoor for a grouped function artifact. The only job here is one-way derivation of the transitional `legacy_fn_def` artifact from those already-grouped quoted roots.

Landed in f83105a72.

What changed:
- function discovery now notes a lazy  authoritative source fact first
-  derives  on demand from grouped quoted source instead of eager old-parser authority
- downstream compatibility now flows through , a compiler2-owned one-way decoder from grouped quoted roots
- the quoted front door and grouped-surface readers were tightened so the lazy seam stays honest (/ attachment, extern-symbol folding vs bitstring parsing, remote operator capture shape)

Verification:
- cargo fmt --all
- cargo check --all
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test --lib compiler2::legacy_fn_def_test:: -- --nocapture
- cargo test --lib

title: Compiler2 legacy_fn_def: derive downstream fn input from grouped quoted roots
